prompt: Using ProteinMPNN, design a panel of stabilised sequences for the neuraminidase N1 catalytic domain backbone from Influenza A H1N1 (PDB 3NSS) for use in recombinant antigen production and structure-based inhibitor screening. Apply thermostability constraints to increase expression yield in E. coli at 37°C. outline a computational approach covering: (1) backbone preparation with bound sialic acid-mimetic inhibitor as fixed context, (2) ProteinMPNN sampling with active-site residue mask (Arg118, Arg152, Arg224 fixed), (3) sequence diversity assessment by Hamming distance clustering, (4) ESMFold validation and predicted stability ranking. Discuss trade-offs between thermostability mutations and catalytic activity retention.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05) and RCSB PDB (3NSS); target: Neuraminidase N1; organism: Influenza A virus (H1N1 reference); catalogue version v1.0
